headline: Seven repos shipped interface polish, proof artifacts, and workflow hardening across 15 archive-worthy updates.
subhead: Rajeevg.com led with dashboard audits and analytics reporting, while Hackathon Voting Prototype hardened event-day readiness and Workflow Garden refreshed diary outputs.

refreshGate.reason: A forced refresh was requested for the diary archive.

days.label: Today
days.summary: Rajeevg.com audited dashboard denominators and published production proof, Openrouter Model Workbook Maintainer V2 masked workflow secrets after Infisical fetch, and Analytics Stack Forensics Archive polished the README and added release tooling.
days.spotlight: Rajeevg.com closed a no-ticket audit that clarified dashboard denominators and moved the calibrated badge off backend copy, then published production proof to GitHub.

entries.title: Dashboard denominator audit closes with production proof and label clarification
entries.summary: Rajeevg.com finished a no-ticket audit that hardened dashboard denominators, clarified labels, and moved the calibrated badge off backend copy. Production proof now lives on GitHub alongside the plain-language README and operator docs.
entries.narrative: Nineteen commits touched 33 files, including the README, the marketing-measurement explainer, operator docs, and proof artifacts. The audit tightened dashboard denominators, clarified labels, and relocated the calibrated badge so backend copy no longer carries interface weight.
entries.whyItMatters: Visitors can now inspect the dashboard logic and proof trail instead of trusting undocumented claims, and operators inherit clearer labels that reduce interpretation risk.
entries.exploreNext: Visit the audit concept page to understand how responsiveness, accessibility, and quality checks combine into a single gate.

entries.title: Workflow secrets now masked after Infisical fetch, deprecated action removed
entries.summary: Openrouter Model Workbook Maintainer V2 recorded Vercel hardening proof, masked workflow secrets immediately after Infisical fetch, and removed the deprecated Infisical action from the daily refresh. The latest commit and GitHub repo link document the changes.
entries.narrative: Eighteen commits touched 57 files, including CI config, the daily refresh workflow, the README, default profiles, and mapping diagnostics. The team fixed deploy verification, aligned Infisical runtime behavior, and ensured secrets never leak into logs.
entries.whyItMatters: Operators can now trace secret handling from fetch to mask in the public workflow, and the deprecated action removal prevents future runtime drift.
entries.exploreNext: Read the OpenRouter Model Workbook Maintainer V2 project page to see how deterministic pipelines join pricing, coverage, and benchmark data.

mask workflow secrets after Infisical fetch
remove deprecated Infisical action from refresh
fix deploy verification and Infisical runtime

entries.title: Archive README polished, release bundle tooling added for v0.1.0
entries.summary: Analytics Stack Forensics Archive polished the README, added release bundle tooling, and archived the analytics stack forensics evidence pack. The pull request template, gitignore, and v0.1.0 manifest now live in the repo.
entries.narrative: Two commits touched 44 files, including the pull request template, gitignore, README, v0.1.0 release folder, automation scripts, and manifest. The polish pass tightened language, and the new tooling automates bundle creation for future releases.
entries.whyItMatters: Visitors can now download a versioned evidence pack with a single command, and the polished README removes ambiguity about what the archive contains.
entries.exploreNext: Visit the audit concept page to see how polish fits into the final quality gate before a page ships.

days.label: Wed 25 Mar
days.summary: Rajeevg.com proved BigQuery export state and simplified hackathon dashboards, Hackathon Voting Prototype exposed a public vote summary and hardened mobile scoreboard interactions, and Automation Hub implemented a codification action tracking loop.
days.spotlight: Rajeevg.com reconciled persisted votes against the live source of truth, then published BigQuery export proof and simplified hackathon reporting dashboards.

entries.title: BigQuery export state proved, hackathon dashboards simplified and reconciled
entries.summary: Rajeevg.com proved BigQuery export state, simplified hackathon dashboards, aligned the fallback entry readout, and reconciled persisted votes against the live source of truth. Live evidence and proof artifacts now document the reporting repair.
entries.narrative: Eight commits touched 31 files, including the README, operator docs, live evidence, proof artifacts, project detail pages, and reusable interface pieces. The team repaired hackathon reporting dashboards, aligned BigQuery fallback logic, and published proof that persisted votes match the live source.
entries.whyItMatters: Visitors can now inspect the BigQuery export state and vote reconciliation proof instead of trusting backend claims, and operators inherit simpler dashboards that reduce cognitive load.
entries.exploreNext: Visit the audit concept page to understand how proof and validation combine into a single quality gate.

entries.title: Public vote summary exposed, mobile scoreboard interactions hardened
entries.summary: Hackathon Voting Prototype exposed a public vote summary, removed scoreboard initial tiles, hardened mobile scoreboard interactions, and redirected proof tickets when a session already exists. The environment contract and README document the changes.
entries.narrative: Eight commits touched 26 files, including the environment contract, README, route config, page components, consent manager, and results dashboard. The team removed initial tiles that confused mobile users, hardened tap interactions, and added a redirect so proof tickets never collide with active sessions.
entries.whyItMatters: Visitors can now see vote summaries without logging in, and mobile users inherit a scoreboard that responds predictably to taps instead of requiring guesswork.
entries.exploreNext: Read the proof concept page to see how inspectable evidence replaces trust-based claims.

entries.title: Codification action tracking loop implemented, operator context collapsed by default
entries.summary: Automation Hub implemented a codification action tracking loop, collapsed operator context by default, clarified sidebar hierarchy for automation views, and ran a full UI audit. The README, operator docs, automation scripts, and CLI now reflect the changes.
entries.narrative: Twelve commits touched 21 files, including the README, operator docs, automation scripts, CLI, codification tracking module, and dashboard. The team limited collapsed operator context to one sentence, clarified sidebar hierarchy, and tightened task-first layouts after the audit.
entries.whyItMatters: Operators can now track codification actions in a loop instead of manually polling logs, and the collapsed context keeps the interface scannable without hiding critical detail.
entries.exploreNext: Read the agent skills article to see how reusable instruction packs keep workflows consistent across sessions.

days.label: Tue 24 Mar
days.summary: Rajeevg.com aligned hackathon reporting shells and published a GA4 site dashboard, Hackathon Voting Prototype synced post-deploy risk evidence and finalized event-day hardening, and Automation Hub ran a full UI audit and tightened task-first layouts.
days.spotlight: Rajeevg.com published the hackathon engineering post alongside a GA4 site dashboard, then built a fallback for hackathon analytics and aligned reporting shells.

entries.title: Hackathon reporting shells aligned, GA4 site dashboard and engineering post published
entries.summary: Rajeevg.com aligned hackathon reporting shells, added a GA API-backed reporting surface, published the hackathon engineering post and GA4 site dashboard, and built a fallback for hackathon analytics. The README and operator docs now document the stack.
entries.narrative: Twenty-seven commits touched 70 files, including gitignore, the README, three new engineering articles, and operator docs. The team published the consented first-party analytics stack article, the hackathon voting app article, and the GA4 property setup article, then aligned reporting shells and built a BigQuery fallback.
entries.whyItMatters: Visitors can now read the full analytics stack implementation story and inspect the GA4 dashboard, while operators inherit a fallback that keeps reporting alive when the API rate-limits.
entries.exploreNext: Visit the polish concept page to see how the final pass makes a page feel considered instead of merely functional.

entries.title: Full UI audit run, task-first layouts tightened, operator context collapsed
entries.summary: Automation Hub ran a full UI audit, tightened task-first layouts, collapsed operator context by default, and clarified sidebar hierarchy for automation views. The pull request template, gitignore, repo workflow guide, and README now reflect the changes.
entries.narrative: Twenty commits touched 81 files, including the pull request template, gitignore, repo workflow guide, README, operator docs, and pyproject config. The team limited collapsed operator context to one sentence, clarified sidebar hierarchy, and tightened task-first layouts after the audit.
entries.whyItMatters: Operators can now scan the interface without expanding context blocks, and the clarified sidebar hierarchy reduces navigation guesswork.
entries.exploreNext: Visit the audit concept page to see how the final quality gate checks responsiveness, accessibility, and obvious risks.

entries.title: Analytics stack implementation article published, end-to-end GA4 and GTM stack completed
entries.summary: Rajeevg.com published the analytics stack implementation article, completed the end-to-end GA4 and GTM stack, preserved deployed portfolio and mermaid updates, and added a public portfolio route with a project article. The README and operator docs now document the stack.
entries.narrative: Four commits touched 37 files, including gitignore, the README, the Git workflow for beginners article, the analytics stack article, the projects portfolio page, and operator docs. The team completed the GA4 property setup, GTM container config, and consent manager integration, then published the full implementation story.
entries.whyItMatters: Visitors can now read the complete analytics stack implementation story and inspect the GA4 property setup, while operators inherit a documented stack that removes guesswork from future changes.
entries.exploreNext: Visit the proof concept page to see how inspectable evidence replaces trust-based claims.

entries.title: Public load shielded, event-day readiness hardened, GitHub runbook added
entries.summary: Hackathon Voting Prototype shielded public load and transient database spikes, hardened event-day readiness, added a GitHub runbook for event operations, and hardened voting readiness with production auth proof. The environment contract and README document the changes.
entries.narrative: Six commits touched 139 files, including the environment contract, gitignore, README, code modules, layout, and page components. The team added rate-limiting middleware, connection pooling, and a GitHub runbook that documents event-day operations, then published production auth proof.
entries.whyItMatters: Visitors can now inspect the event-day runbook and production auth proof on GitHub, and operators inherit a system that survives transient load spikes without manual intervention.
entries.exploreNext: Visit the agent skills article to see how reusable instruction packs keep workflows consistent across sessions.

entries.title: Demo redesigned into mission-control interface, slow generations retried on faster lane
entries.summary: Multi Agent Orchestration Demo redesigned the interface into a mission-control layout, retried slow generations on a faster model lane, and hardened the demo for public shipment. The pull request template, gitignore, Infisical config, and README now reflect the changes.
entries.narrative: Three commits touched 48 files, including the pull request template, gitignore, Infisical config, repo workflow guide, CLAUDE instructions, and README. The team redesigned the demo into a mission-control interface that shows agent state in real time, then added a retry mechanism that switches to a faster model lane when generations stall.
entries.whyItMatters: Visitors can now watch agent orchestration unfold in a mission-control interface instead of reading logs, and the retry mechanism keeps the demo responsive when slow models stall.
entries.exploreNext: Visit the PRD concept page to see how product intent gets documented before implementation starts.

entries.searchText: Multi Agent Orchestration Demo redesigned the interface into a mission-control layout, retried slow generations on a faster model lane, and hardened the demo for public shipment. The pull request template, gitignore, Infisical config, and README now reflect the changes. Three commits touched 48 files, including the pull request template, gitignore, Infisical config, repo workflow guide, CLAUDE instructions, and README. The team redesigned the demo into a mission-control interface that shows agent state in real time, then added a retry mechanism that switches to a faster model lane when generations stall. Visitors can now watch agent orchestration unfold in a mission-control interface instead of reading logs, and the retry mechanism keeps the demo responsive when slow models stall. Visit the PRD concept page to see how product intent gets documented before implementation starts. Multi Agent Orchestration Demo pull request template gitignore infisical the repo workflow guide Redesign the demo into a mission-control interface Retry slow generations on a faster model lane Harden and ship multi-agent orchestration demo pull request template PRD proof What proof means: design-proof vs acceptance-proof Why one issue, one branch, and one PR matters

days.summary: Workflow Garden refreshed diary publishable outputs and repaired the publish loop, Rajeevg.com kept mermaid diagrams readable on narrow screens and simplified public content, and Openrouter Model Workbook Maintainer V2 relaxed rate-limit fallback matching and aligned local bootstrap messaging.

entries.title: Diary publishable outputs refreshed, publish loop repaired, detail deepened
entries.summary: Workflow Garden refreshed diary publishable outputs, repaired the publish loop, and deepened diary detail and copy quality. The environment contract, README, project pages, operator docs, and proof artifacts now document the changes, and the GitHub repo, latest commit, live site, and proof trail link to the evidence.
entries.narrative: Four commits touched 21 files, including the environment contract, README, project pages, operator docs, acceptance proof, and proof artifacts. The team repaired the publish loop that broke when the diary schema changed, then deepened diary detail and tightened copy quality.
entries.whyItMatters: Visitors can now inspect the diary publish loop and proof trail on GitHub, and operators inherit a system that publishes diary updates without manual intervention.
entries.exploreNext: Visit the Workflow Garden project page to see how an issue-driven AI workflow becomes something a visitor can understand.

entries.title: Mermaid diagrams kept readable on narrow screens, public content simplified
entries.summary: Rajeevg.com kept mermaid diagrams readable on narrow screens, removed a duplicate article h1, simplified public content, and refreshed the about page. The README and four article pages now reflect the changes.
entries.narrative: Eight commits touched 20 files, including the README, Docker gcloud deployment article, AI pilots to business value article, hello world article, Windsurf GPT-5 prompts article, and next.config. The team added responsive styles that keep mermaid diagrams readable on narrow screens, removed a duplicate h1 that broke semantic structure, and simplified public content.
entries.whyItMatters: Visitors can now read mermaid diagrams on mobile without horizontal scrolling, and the simplified content removes ambiguity about what the site offers.
entries.exploreNext: Visit the proof concept page to see how design-proof and acceptance-proof combine into a single quality gate.

entries.title: Rate-limit fallback match relaxed, daily refresh falls back to repo secrets
entries.summary: Openrouter Model Workbook Maintainer V2 relaxed rate-limit fallback matching, added a rate-limit fallback for daily refresh, made daily refresh fall back to repo secrets, and aligned local bootstrap messaging with Infisical. The environment contract, CI config, daily refresh workflow, and gitignore now document the changes, and the GitHub repo and latest commit link to the evidence.
entries.narrative: Seven commits touched 83 files, including the environment contract, CI config, daily refresh workflow, gitignore, agent task instructions, and Makefile. The team relaxed rate-limit fallback matching so the workflow survives transient API errors, then made daily refresh fall back to repo secrets when Infisical is unavailable.
entries.whyItMatters: Operators can now trace fallback logic from rate-limit detection to repo secret usage in the public workflow, and the aligned bootstrap messaging removes confusion about when Infisical is required.
entries.exploreNext: Visit the OpenRouter Model Workbook Maintainer V2 project page to see how deterministic pipelines join pricing, coverage, and benchmark data.
